# elecRetrns — Recent Mexican Election Vote Returns

> Vote returns for Mexican elections since the 1960s–70s, compiled by Eric
> Magar (ITAM). Municipal (ayuntamiento), federal deputy, senate,
> gubernatorial and presidential races, at municipality/district/precinct
> level. MIT license. Cite as: Magar, E. (2018) Recent Mexican election vote
> returns repository, https://github.com/emagar/elecReturns

## How the data is organized

File names encode office + observation unit + years covered:
- Office: ay=ayuntamiento (municipal), df=federal deputies, se=senators,
  pr=president, dl=state deputies, go=governor.
- Unit: mu=municipality, ed=state, df=federal district, se=precinct
  (sección), ca=polling station (casilla), de=demarcación (Nayarit only).
- Example: `data/aymu1970-on.coalAgg.csv` = municipal races, municipality
  level, 1970 to present, coalition votes aggregated by candidate.
- `coalAgg` vs `coalSplit`: same elections; coalAgg reports one vote total
  per candidate (coalition votes merged), coalSplit divides coalition votes
  among member parties where the source permits.

## Key files (raw CSV, directly downloadable)

- Municipal returns (wide format, one row per election, votes in columns
  v01/l01..v23/l23 where vNN=votes and lNN=party label of candidate NN):
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/aymu1970-on.coalAgg.csv
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/aymu1970-on.coalSplit.csv
- Elected municipal presidents (names, reelection status, gender):
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/aymu1989-on.incumbents.csv
- Federal deputies by district since 1979:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/dfdf1979-on.coalAgg.csv
- Governors since 1961:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/goed1961-on.csv
- President by district (2006-on) and by state (1964-on):
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/prdf2006-on.csv
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emagar/elecRetrns/master/data/pred1964-on.csv

## Tidy version (RECOMMENDED for analysis and AI assistants)

Long-format municipal returns — one row per candidacy-election with fixed
columns (emm, yr, edon, inegi, mun, partido, votos, share, rank, win, mg,
efec, lisnom, status). Much easier to filter and aggregate than the wide
files. Available in this fork:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RazoR-28/elecRetrns/master/Version%20Rodrigo/shiny-app/aymu1970-on.tidy.csv
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RazoR-28/elecRetrns/master/Version%20Rodrigo/shiny-app/aymu1970-on.coalSplit.tidy.csv

Machine-readable codebook (variable, description, type, files):
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RazoR-28/elecRetrns/master/codebook.csv

Interactive explorer (maps, time series, CSV/Excel export):
https://elecrazo.shinyapps.io/elecretrns/

## Things an assistant must know before answering with this data

- `efec` (effective vote) is the denominator for vote shares: raw votes
  minus write-ins and invalid ballots.
- In wide files the party behind column v01 CHANGES row by row — always
  read the matching lNN label. Never assume column position = party.
- `share` and `mg` (winner margin) are proportions in 0–1.
- Some municipality-years have TWO records (annulled + extraordinary
  election, or first round + runoff). Column `status` disambiguates:
  prefer ok > extra > ok--to-runoff; ignore cancelled/appointed when an
  alternative exists.
- Municipal `inegi` code = state(2 digits)*1000 + municipality(3 digits).
- Oaxaca has hundreds of municipalities electing by usos y costumbres
  (no party competition) — absent years there are normal, not missing data.
- Known source anomaly: jal-18.118 (Yahualica, Jalisco 2021) vote sum
  differs from efec by 198 votes.
- Party label glossary (main): pan, pri, prd, morena, pvem, pt, mc,
  pna/panal (Nueva Alianza), pes, conve/mc (Convergencia→Movimiento
  Ciudadano), pfcrn, parm, pps, pdm, psum, pst, fxm, rsp, indep
  (independent). Coalition labels join parties with hyphens: "pri-pvem-pna".

## Full documentation

- README with complete codebook: https://github.com/emagar/elecRetrns
- Research note describing the municipal data:
  https://github.com/emagar/elecRetrns/tree/master/papers/data-descriptives
